name: Olive (food medium)
description: Food-derived medium: metabolizable components of Olive (Olea europaea) [Vegetables] from FooDB, plus a standard M9 mineral base. Organic components use presence-based uptake bounds; FooDB content retained. Measured compounds with no BiGG exchange are listed under uncovered.

provenance.citation: FooDB v2020-04-07 (Wishart DS et al., foodb.ca). Content sources: DTU, DUKE, PHENOL EXPLORER, USDA.
provenance.notes: Organic components mapped from FooDB compounds to BiGG via InChIKey/name. Mineral base added. Bounds presence-based; foodb_content retained. Unmapped measured compounds kept in uncovered (v2 completeness fix). IMPORTANT: composition values are taken from the archived FooDB 2020-04-07 CSV data release; FooDB's live food page no longer displays this bulk composition, so it will appear empty. Verify individual compounds via the per-component cross-reference links. Underlying content was aggregated by FooDB from USDA / DTU / Phenol Explorer.
provenance.verification: expert-curated (multi-source food composition; growth-relevant profile completed, FooDB noise removed)
curation_sources: USDA FooDData Central FDC 169094 (Olives, ripe, canned, small-extra large; SR Legacy NDB 09193)
Marsilio V. et al. 2001, Food Chemistry 72(4):485-490, doi:10.1016/S0308-8146(00)00268-5 (sugars & polyols of olive fruit)
MDPI Agriculture 2022, 12(9):1347 (ripening effect on sugars & phenolics, cv. Leccino)
Nergiz C. & Ergunel P.G. 2010, Czech J. Food Sci. 28(3):202-205, doi:10.17221/1379-CJFS (organic acids in olive fruit by HPLC)
International Olive Council COI/T.15/NC No 3/Rev.16 (2021) (olive-oil fatty-acid ranges)
Review: total olive-pulp acidity 4-10 g/kg expressed as citric acid
provenance.curation_notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources of olive are (1) OLIVE OIL / triacylglycerols, overwhelmingly oleate, and (2) the free sugars + mannitol of the pulp; organic acids (citric>malic) are the main previously-missing metabolites. Prior FooDB profile lacked all free sugars, mannitol and organic acids and under-specified the oil. Micronutrient/amino-acid/fatty-acid panel is from USDA canned ripe olives (09193); note that canning/brining elevates Na and Fe and leaches sugars/K, so sugar and organic-acid a
curation_added: citric acid (0.15 g/100g, dominant organic acid)
total olive oil / triacylglycerol quantified (~15 g/100g, dominant carbon source)

curation_removed.name: Retinol
curation_removed.reason: Preformed (animal) vitamin A; olive is a plant and contains provitamin-A carotenoids (beta-carotene/cryptoxanthin), not retinol. USDA 09193 retinol = 0. Keep beta-carotene instead.

curation_removed.name: 3-4-Dihydroxyphenylacetate
curation_removed.reason: DOPAC, a mammalian dopamine catabolite; FooDB pathway-predicted, not a measured olive constituent (genuine olive catechols are hydroxytyrosol/DHPEA compounds, kept as uncovered).

curation_removed.name: 3-Methoxy-4-hydroxyphenylacetic acid
curation_removed.reason: Homovanillic acid, a mammalian dopamine metabolite; FooDB-predicted, biologically implausible as an olive nutrient.

curation_removed.name: Homoveratric acid
curation_removed.reason: 3,4-dimethoxyphenylacetic acid, a mammalian catecholamine metabolite; FooDB-predicted, implausible in olive fruit.

curation_removed.name: D-Serine
curation_removed.reason: D-enantiomer; plant/food protein amino acids are L-forms. Replaced with L-serine (USDA).
